hypothesis: Mutating position 1 to a bulky hydrophobic residue (e.g., Leu or Ile) will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by improving complementarity with the SSTR2-specific ECL2 pocket while reducing off-target SSTR3/5 binding. Simultaneously, mutating position 12 to a charged residue (e.g., Lys) will improve electrostatic complementarity with SSTR2 TM6 while disrupting off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are failing primarily due to structural issues, not selectivity. Tighter structural constraints and a larger candidate pool are needed to identify a viable SSTR2-selective binder.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current clash score threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with poor packing and structural instability. Tightening the clash score will enforce better geometry and reduce false positives.

The low number of candidates increases the risk of missing high-quality designs. Expanding the candidate pool will improve diversity and increase the chances of finding a structurally valid, selective binder.
